initial_program:
% ----------  fullcolumn and fullrow detection  ----------
fullCol(C)   :- #count{R : input(R,C,V), V != 5} = 0.
fullRow(R)   :- #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 5} = 0.

% ----------  row side (above / below a full row) ----------
% If there is at least one full row, rows above it are top, rows below are bottom.
% If there is no full row, both predicates become true for every row  this gives the
% same colour for all rows (the intended behaviour for the examples without a full row).
topSide(R)   :- #count{F : fullRow(F), F > R} > 0.
topSide(R)   :- #count{F : fullRow(F)} = 0.

bottomSide(R) :- #count{F : fullRow(F), F < R} > 0.
bottomSide(R) :- #count{F : fullRow(F)} = 0.

% ----------  column region (left / middle / right of the two full columns) ----------
colLeft(C)   :- #count{F : fullCol(F), F <= C} = 0.
colMid(C)    :- #count{F : fullCol(F), F < C} > 0, #count{F : fullCol(F), F > C} > 0.
colRight(C)  :- #count{F : fullCol(F), F >= C} = 0.

% ----------  output for the vertical full5 walls ----------
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,_), fullCol(C).

% ----------  output for the horizontal full5 wall ----------
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,_), fullRow(R).

% ----------  left side, top ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colLeft(C),
    topSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colLeft(C2), topSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  left side, bottom ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colLeft(C),
    bottomSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colLeft(C2), bottomSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  middle side, top ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colMid(C),
    topSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colMid(C2), topSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  middle side, bottom ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colMid(C),
    bottomSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colMid(C2), bottomSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  right side, top ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colRight(C),
    topSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colRight(C2), topSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  right side, bottom ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colRight(C),
    bottomSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colRight(C2), bottomSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  each input cell must receive exactly one colour ----------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ----------  fullcolumn and fullrow detection  ----------
fullCol(C)   :- #count{R : input(R,C,V), V != 5} = 0.
fullRow(R)   :- #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 5} = 0.

% ----------  row side (above / below a full row) ----------
% If there is at least one full row, rows above it are top, rows below are bottom.
% If there is no full row, both predicates become true for every row  this gives the
% same colour for all rows (the intended behaviour for the examples without a full row).
topSide(R)   :- #count{F : fullRow(F), F > R} > 0.
topSide(R)   :- #count{F : fullRow(F)} = 0.

bottomSide(R) :- #count{F : fullRow(F), F < R} > 0.
bottomSide(R) :- #count{F : fullRow(F)} = 0.

% ----------  column region (left / middle / right of the two full columns) ----------
colLeft(C)   :- #count{F : fullCol(F), F <= C} = 0.
colMid(C)    :- #count{F : fullCol(F), F < C} > 0, #count{F : fullCol(F), F > C} > 0.
colRight(C)  :- #count{F : fullCol(F), F >= C} = 0.

% ----------  output for the vertical full5 walls ----------
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,_), fullCol(C).

% ----------  output for the horizontal full5 wall ----------
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,_), fullRow(R).

% ----------  left side, top ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colLeft(C),
    topSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colLeft(C2), topSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  left side, bottom ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colLeft(C),
    bottomSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colLeft(C2), bottomSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  middle side, top ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colMid(C),
    topSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colMid(C2), topSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  middle side, bottom ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colMid(C),
    bottomSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colMid(C2), bottomSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  right side, top ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colRight(C),
    topSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colRight(C2), topSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  right side, bottom ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colRight(C),
    bottomSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colRight(C2), bottomSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  each input cell must receive exactly one colour ----------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% ----------  fullcolumn and fullrow detection  ----------
fullCol(C)   :- #count{R : input(R,C,V), V != 5} = 0.
fullRow(R)   :- #count{C : input(R,C,V), V != 5} = 0.

% ----------  row side (above / below a full row) ----------
% If there is at least one full row, rows above it are top, rows below are bottom.
% If there is no full row, both predicates become true for every row  this gives the
% same colour for all rows (the intended behaviour for the examples without a full row).
topSide(R)   :- #count{F : fullRow(F), F > R} > 0.
topSide(R)   :- #count{F : fullRow(F)} = 0.

bottomSide(R) :- #count{F : fullRow(F), F < R} > 0.
bottomSide(R) :- #count{F : fullRow(F)} = 0.

% ----------  column region (left / middle / right of the two full columns) ----------
colLeft(C)   :- #count{F : fullCol(F), F <= C} = 0.
colMid(C)    :- #count{F : fullCol(F), F < C} > 0, #count{F : fullCol(F), F > C} > 0.
colRight(C)  :- #count{F : fullCol(F), F >= C} = 0.

% ----------  output for the vertical full5 walls ----------
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,_), fullCol(C).

% ----------  output for the horizontal full5 wall ----------
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,_), fullRow(R).

% ----------  left side, top ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colLeft(C),
    topSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colLeft(C2), topSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  left side, bottom ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colLeft(C),
    bottomSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colLeft(C2), bottomSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  middle side, top ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colMid(C),
    topSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colMid(C2), topSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  middle side, bottom ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colMid(C),
    bottomSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colMid(C2), bottomSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  right side, top ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colRight(C),
    topSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colRight(C2), topSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  right side, bottom ----------
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,_),
    colRight(C),
    bottomSide(R),
    Color = 5 + #max{V : input(R2,C2,V), colRight(C2), bottomSide(R2), V != 5}.

% ----------  each input cell must receive exactly one colour ----------
output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% Exactly one answer set
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
